LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CATHY DAVIS, BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES AND CATHY DAVIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 31, 2019. DEFENDANTS' SEPARATE STATEMENT REFERENCES THE DECLARATION OF CATHY DAVIS WITH ATTACHED RESIGNATION LETTER AND LETTER ACCEPTING THE RESIGNATION. HOWEVER, THE DAVIS DECLARATION WAS NOT INCLUDED WITH DEFENDANTS' MOVING PAPERS. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF FELISIA THIBODEAUX FAILS TO ATTACH TO THE ATTORNEY DECLARATION ALL OF THE EXCERPTS OF DEPOSITION TESTIMONY ON WHICH SHE RELIES TO DEFEND AGAINST THIS MOTION. BOTH PARTIES ARE INSTRUCTED TO FILE THEIR SUPPORTING EVIDENCE BY 5:00 P.M. ON MAY 22, 2019 TO GIVE THE COURT THE OPPORTUNITY TO REVIEW IT IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING. PLAINTIFF IS URGED TO ATTACH THE ENTIRETY OF THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY RATHER THAN EXCERPTS IN ORDER TO AVOID FURTHER OMISSIONS OF EVIDENCE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial, Or In The Alternative An Order Shortening Time On Motion To Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Off Calendar

Defendants Bayview Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services And Cathy Davis' Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alyssa Villanueva, Esq. 510-897-9966 of Benjamin Law Group for Plaintiff.
E. Forrest Shryock Jr., 415-398-0200 of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Bayview Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services And Cathy Davis' Motion For Summary Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendants Bayview Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services and Cathy Davis' summary judgment motion is DENIED. An adverse employment action is an essential element of each of plaintiff Felisia Thibodeaux's claims and thus is the focus of defendants' motion. Plaintiff's supposed inability to prove causation was argued for the first time in reply, so that argument is not considered.

A plain reading of plaintiff's resignation letter indicates she resigned. (Davis. Dec. Ex. A.) However, while plaintiff's evidence is weak, it must be construed liberally. (Binder v. Aetna Life Ins. Co. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 832, 838.) And the deposition excerpts combine with the letter to create triable issues of fact on adverse employment action and constructive discharge. There are, for instance, disputes as to whether plaintiff's efforts to investigate Frank Williams and protect Shereena Abdullah resulted in plaintiff being subjected to retaliation and a hostile work environment by Davis. Examples include undermining plaintiff's authority, hostile interactions and the termination of plaintiff's direct report, Jose Reyes. (UMF 23, 25-26, 28, 31, 40, 42-43; Davis Dec. Ex. A.) While isolated social slights and exhibitions of anger may not on their own establish retaliation, a pattern of such acts, combined with efforts to undermine plaintiff's functioning in the workplace, may suffice. (Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1053-54, 1056.) It is for a finder of fact to decide whether Davis retaliated and whether plaintiff's resignation letter was motivated by intolerable work conditions reaching the level of an adverse employment action.

Finally, constructive discharge is alleged in the complaint (26), so it provides a basis for plaintiff's allegations of adverse employment action. While not pled as its own cause of action, constructive discharge supports the retaliation and wrongful termination claims as it may amount to termination as a matter of law. (Turner v. Anheuser-Busch, Inc. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 1238, 1244-45; Steele v. Youthful Offender Parole Bd. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1253.) Unlike in Featherstone v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 1150, 1161, there is evidence to suggest that employer misconduct prompted plaintiff to resign.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Bayciew Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services And Cathy Davis Notice And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CATHY DAVIS, BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT MULTIPURPOSE SENIOR SERVICES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendants Bayview Hunters Point Multipurpose Senior Services and Cathy Davis' motion for summary judgment is CONTINUED to May 31, 2019. Defendants' separate statement references the Declaration of Cathy Davis with attached resignation letter and letter accepting the resignation. However, the Davis Declaration was not included with Defendants' moving papers. Furthermore, Plaintiff Felisia Thibodeaux fails to attach to the attorney declaration all of the excerpts of deposition testimony on which she relies to defend against this motion. Both parties are instructed to file their supporting evidence by 5:00 p.m. on May 22, 2019 to give the Court the opportunity to review it in advance of the hearing. Plaintiff is urged to attach the entirety of the deposition testimony rather than excerpts in order to avoid further omissions of evidence.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
